edges.description: Mosaic FGFR1/RAS-MAPK activation in developing tissues produces CNS lipomatous malformations.
edges.source: Somatic FGFR1 gain-of-function in developing tissues

edges.description: Mosaic FGFR1/RAS-MAPK activation in developing cutaneous tissues produces fatty nevi and related skin hamartomas.
edges.source: Somatic FGFR1 gain-of-function in developing tissues

edges.description: Mosaic FGFR1/RAS-MAPK activation in ocular tissues contributes to benign ocular tumors/choristomas.
edges.source: Somatic FGFR1 gain-of-function in developing tissues

edges.description: CNS lipomas and malformations can produce seizures.

edges.description: CNS lipomas and malformations can impair neurodevelopment.

nodes.description: Hairless fatty tissue nevus of the scalp (nevus psiloliparus) and facial hamartomas are characteristic cutaneous features, typically unilateral.

evidence.snippet: It is characterized by unilateral ocular, cutaneous and central nervous system anomalies.
well-demarcated hairless fatty nevus on the scalp

nodes.description: Intellectual disability can occur in ECCL with central nervous system lipomas and malformations.
Intracerebral and intraspinal lipomas are characteristic CNS manifestations. They may cause ventriculomegaly and cerebral atrophy.

evidence.snippet: Key clinical features include hairless fatty tissue nevus of the scalp, choristoma of the eye and intraspinal and intracerebral lipomas.

nodes.description: Choristoma (dermoid or lipodermoid) of the eye, typically epibulbar, is a key diagnostic feature of ECCL. Limbal dermoids are the most common ocular manifestation.

evidence.snippet: Key clinical features include hairless fatty tissue nevus of the scalp, choristoma of the eye and intraspinal and intracerebral lipomas.

nodes.description: Seizures can occur as a neurologic consequence of ECCL-associated CNS malformations.

Postzygotic activating mutations in FGFR1 occur during early embryonic development, leading to mosaic overactivation of FGFR1 signaling in affected tissues. This drives aberrant proliferation of mesenchymal and neural crest-derived cells, producing lipomas, cutaneous hamartomas, and CNS anomalies in a mosaic distribution. ECCL shares significant phenotypic overlap with mosaic RASopathies due to convergent activation of the RAS-MAPK pathway.
nodes.id: Somatic FGFR1 gain-of-function in developing tissues

evidence.snippet: Encephalocraniocutaneous lipomatosis (ECCL) is a rare neurocutaneous disorder caused by somatic FGFR1 and KRAS variants.
next-generation sequencing of affected tissue identified a pathologic FGFR1 p.K656E variant, thereby establishing a molecular diagnosis.

nodes.description: Activating missense variant in the kinase domain of FGFR1 identified in affected tissue.
